insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing for more specific sales channel context in queries. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query, including channel-specific pricing and inventory.
insight.developer_impact: Developers should update their Storefront API queries to utilize the new @inContext directive with the channelId argument. This will enable more accurate and channel-specific data retrieval. When migrating, ensure that the correct channelId is used for each query, and test thoroughly to avoid any errors. The GraphQL Admin API can be used to retrieve the channelId for a specific sales channel.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and channel-specific data, leading to a better overall shopping experience. This update will also enable more precise inventory management and pricing across different sales channels.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and accuracy in querying sales channel-specific data, which is crucial for merchants with multiple sales channels. It also aligns with Shopify's efforts to enhance the Storefront API and provide a better developer experience.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate for apps storing cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2 introduces non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and the ability to bypass confirmation prompts during hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirements have been updated to include the use of a unique name for each app to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports the channelId argument for applying sales channel context to queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components in cart line item data.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1 improves command metadata handling and preserves actionable build errors during h2 deploy.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects.

updates.title: skeleton@2026.4.5
updates.summary: skeleton@2026.4.5 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects.

updates.title: POS Extensions now supports a background extension target
updates.summary: POS Extensions now supports a background extension target for observing POS events and running background logic.

recap.headline: Shopify updates require mTLS certificates, new Hydrogen features, and App Store requirement changes

themes.title: Security Updates
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and apps storing cardholder data must comply. Additionally, the `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` packages have been updated with patch changes.

themes.title: Hydrogen and CLI Updates
themes.detail: New versions of `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` have been released with features such as non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags, and explicit `~ app` aliases in new Hydrogen projects. The CLI has also been updated with new store commands.

themes.title: API and App Store Changes
themes.detail: The Storefront API now supports `channelId` in the `@inContext` directive, and new fields have been added to the `ExchangeLineItem` type. The App Store has updated its requirements, including the use of unique app names, and POS UI Extensions now include discount allocations to bundle components.

actions.text: Update apps to use mTLS certificates for card deposit endpoint
Review and comply with updated App Store requirements
Test and implement new Hydrogen features and CLI updates
